channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Harnessing AI: A NetApp perspective
item.description: We take pride in our heritage at NetApp. Our heritage is rooted in developing innovative solutions that address the challenges of storing, managing, and protecting data in a complex IT environment.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Nile unwraps NaaS security features for enterprise customers
item.description: Network-as-a-service startup Nile has added new zero-trust capabilities and tapped Microsoft and Palo Alto Networks to bolster its enterprise security services. Nile was founded by former Cisco CEO John Chambers and Pankaj Patel, Cisco’s former chief development officer.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Bitcoin is at the doorstep of $100,000 as post-election rally rolls on
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Bitcoin topped $98,000 for the first time Thursday, extending a streak of record after record highs since the U.S. presidential election. The cryptocurrency has rocketed more than 40% in just two weeks.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Silicon Valley tech boom lifts California’s dreary budget view
item.description: A gauge of the so-called Magnificent Seven mega tech companies — Alphabet, Amazon.com Inc., Apple, Meta, Microsoft Corp., Nvidia and Tesla Inc. — is up 56% so far this year, more than twice the advance of the S&P 500 Index.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: US Justice Department seeks Google Chrome sale to curb monopoly
item.description: The US Justice Department and a group of states proposed major changes to Alphabet Inc.’s Google — including a forced sale of the company’s Chrome web browser — after a landmark ruling that the tech giant illegally monopolized online search.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Global AI regulations: Beyond the U.S. and Europe
item.description: An earlier article described emerging AI regulations for the U.S. and Europe. Building on that perspective, this article describes examples of AI regulations in the rest of the world and provides a summary on global AI regulation trends.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Thai court dismisses activist's suit against Israeli spyware producer over lack of evidence
item.description: A Thai court has dismissed a lawsuit brought by a pro-democracy activist who alleged spyware produced by an Israeli tech firm had been used to hack his phone.
item.dc:creator: Jerry Harmer And David Rising, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: DuckDuckGo Urges EU Investigation into Google's Compliance with Digital Markets Act
item.description: Privacy-focused search engine DuckDuckGo calls for three investigations into Google's compliance with the European Union's Digital Markets Act, alleging non-compliance and selective adherence to key obligations.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: CIOs view cost management as possible AI value killer
item.description: CIOs feeling the pressure to deploy successful AI projects have a second concern: that they don’t have the money to pull it off. Ninety percent of CIOs recently surveyed by Gartner say that managing AI costs is limiting their ability to get value from AI.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: US regulators seek to break up Google, forcing Chrome sale as part of monopoly punishment
item.description: U.S. regulators want a federal judge to break up Google to prevent the company from continuing to squash competition through its dominant search engine after a court found it had maintained an abusive monopoly over the past decade.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Class action lawsuit on AI-related discrimination reaches final settlement
item.description: A federal judge has signed off on a settlement agreement Wednesday in a class action lawsuit alleging that an algorithm designed to score rental applicants discriminated on the basis of race and income.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Bedayn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The Baltic undersea cable cutting highlights the internet’s underlying vulnerabilities
item.description: The severing of a key subsea telecoms cable running under Europe’s Baltic Sea has caused a diplomatic incident this week, as both country’s politicians called the act “sabotage”—all while Russia, whom many say is the likely instigator, denies responsibility.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Nvidia is Wall Street's most valuable company. How it got there, by the numbers
item.description: Nvidia has once again turned out quarterly results that exceeded Wall Street's forecasts. The company has seen soaring demand for its semiconductors, which are used to power artificial intelligence applications.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Comcast to spin off cable networks that were once the entertainment giant's star performers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Comcast is spinning off into a new company many of the cable television networks that were once at the entertainment giant's heart as consumers increasingly swap out their cable TV subscriptions for streaming platforms.
item.dc:creator: MATT OTT and WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Iggy Azalea explains why she’s gone all in on crypto
item.description: Rapper and entrepreneur Iggy Azalea’s crypto token $MOTHER isn’t just another “pump and dump” celebrity crypto play—it’s her next act.
item.dc:creator: Robert Safian, Fast Company

item.title: DoD, Hill eye CMMC tax credit for smaller defense contractors
item.description: The CMMC tax credit would be available to companies with 50 or fewer employees. The bill could feature in negotiations over tax cuts next year. The post DoD, Hill eye CMMC tax credit for smaller defense contractors first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: NetOps startup Selector AI wants to solve the network noise problem
item.description: Back in 2019, Kannan Kothandaraman helped to start Selector AI after spending years in the networking trenches at big vendors including Juniper and Cisco.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Australia won't force social media users to share their personal details when child ban takes effect
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australia's communications minister said Wednesday the government won't force social media users to hand over their personal information to tech companies, as children younger than 16 are set to be banned from the platforms.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Must-do privacy settings on your iPhone in iOS 18.1
item.description: Apple's latest iOS update introduces advanced artificial intelligence capabilities, which may be capturing and analyzing sensitive information.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft extends Entra ID to WSL, WinGet
item.description: Microsoft has added new security features to Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL) and the Windows Package Manager (WinGet), including integration with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Active Directory) for identity-based access control.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Germany blames ‘sabotage’ as two undersea fiber cables cut in the Baltic Sea
item.description: The German government has blamed an act of sabotage for the cutting of two important undersea fiber optic cables, one connecting connecting Finland and Germany, and the other linking Sweden and Lithuania, on Sunday and Monday. The first cable break happened at around 8 a.m. GMT (3 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: CMA gives Google’s $2B Anthropic investment the green light
item.description: It took the UK’s Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) less than a month to decide that there is no need to proceed further with a merger investigation after Google’s purchase of a $2 billion stake in Anthropic. In a statement released on Oct.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Study: Chat GPT is better than doctors at diagnosing illness
item.description: A new research study indicates that Open AI’s chatbot Chat GPT-4 is better at diagnosing diseases than human doctors, according to The New York Times. Fifty doctors, a mix of attending physicians and residents, participated in the study; diagnoses were based on evaluations of medical patient cases.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AI chatbot reportedly says to eat deadly mushrooms
item.description: “FungiFriend” was added to a popular mushroom discussion Facebook group and encouraged members to “sauté in butter” a potentially dangerous mushroom.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation
